channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Context Management
channel.description: Latest Context Management news in Context Engineering

item.title: PolicyBank: Evolving Policy Understanding for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.15505v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents operating under organizational policies must comply with authorization constraints typically specified in natural language. In practice, such specifications inevitably contain ambiguities and logical or semantic gaps that cause the agent's ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, memory, LLM, alignment, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Consistency Analysis of Sentiment Predictions using Syntactic & Semantic Context Assessment Summarization (SSAS)
item.description: arXiv:2604.15547v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The fundamental challenge of using Large Language Models (LLMs) for reliable, enterprise-grade analytics, such as sentiment prediction, is the conflict between the LLMs' inherent stochasticity (generative, non-deterministic nature) and the analytical ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, attention, model, analysis, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sequential KV Cache Compression via Probabilistic Language Tries: Beyond the Per-Vector Shannon Limit
item.description: arXiv:2604.15356v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work on KV cache quantization, culminating in TurboQuant, has approached the Shannon entropy limit for per-vector compression of transformer key-value caches. We observe that this limit applies to a strictly weaker problem than the one that act...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, attention, model, RAG, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Illusion of Equivalence: Systematic FP16 Divergence in KV-Cached Autoregressive Inference
item.description: arXiv:2604.15409v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: KV caching is a ubiquitous optimization in autoregressive transformer inference, long presumed to be numerically equivalent to cache-free computation. This assumption fails under standard FP16 precision: cache-ON and cache-OFF execution paths employ d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, attention, model, LLM, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
